title: Interaction-controlled localization in one-dimensional chain: From edges to domain walls
authors: Rahul Samanta, Sudin Ganguly, Santanu K. Maiti
abstract: Using Hartree-Fock mean-field approach, we study the role of on-site ($U$) and extended ($V$) Hubbard interactions on the existence and evolution of edge modes in a half-filled Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain. We analyze the energy spectrum, local probability amplitudes, and site-resolved charge and spin density profiles across topological, critical, and trivial hopping regimes. We find that the localization of bound states is controlled by the ratio $2V/U$, with edge spin-density-wave modes for $U>2V$ and mid-chain charge-density-wave domain walls for $U<2V$, independent of band topology. These results establish the correlation-driven origin of localized states in finite one-dimensional chains.
summary: This paper demonstrates that in an extended Hubbard SSH chain, the spatial character of localized states is determined by the competition between on-site and nearest-neighbor interactions rather than the band topology. It identifies a transition from edge-localized spin-density waves to mid-chain charge-density-wave domain walls based on the $2V/U$ ratio. This establishes that these localized excitations are intrinsic, correlation-driven phenomena.
main_problem: The study investigates whether localized states in a 1D chain are driven by underlying band topology (SSH model) or by electron-electron interactions (Hubbard $U$ and $V$).
main_result: The localization of states is controlled by the ratio $2V/U$ rather than band topology, leading to edge-localized spin-density-wave (SDW) modes for $U > 2V$ and mid-chain charge-density-wave (CDW) domain walls for $U < 2V$.
method: The authors use a self-consistent generalized Hartree-Fock mean-field approximation to solve the Hamiltonian.
model_or_system: A half-filled, one-dimensional Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain described by an extended Hubbard model featuring alternating hopping amplitudes ($t_1, t_2$), on-site repulsion ($U$), and nearest-neighbor interaction ($V$).
key_observables: Energy spectrum (eigenvalues), local probability amplitudes, site-resolved charge density, and site-resolved spin density.
important_parameters: On-site interaction $U$, nearest-neighbor interaction $V$, the ratio $2V/U$, and hopping amplitudes $t_1$ and $t_2$ (covering topological, critical, and trivial regimes).
main_assumptions: The study relies on the Hartree-Fock mean-field approximation, which neglects certain many-body correlation effects beyond the decoupling of interaction terms.
figures_summary: Figures show the energy spectrum, spatial localization of wavefunctions, and density profiles (charge and spin) across different interaction regimes and hopping phases, including the transition from edge modes to mid-chain domain walls.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the SSH-Hubbard model, details the Hartree-Fock method, presents results for different interaction ratios ($U$ vs $V$) across topological, critical, and trivial hopping regimes, and concludes with a summary of interaction-driven localization.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: (Color online). Schematic diagram of the one- dimensional dimerized chain, where t1 (solid line) and t2 (dot- ted line) denote the intra- and inter-unit-cell hopping ampli- tudes, respectively. The white and black balls represent the two sublattices.

FIG. 2: (Color online). (a) Energy eigenvalue spectrum as a function of eigenstate index for a 100-site half-ﬁlled SSH chain with t1 = 0.5 and t2 = 1, in the presence of on-site Hubbard interaction U = 0.8. The two in-gap states are marked by red and green dots. The insets show the corresponding local probability amplitudes |ψi|2 for these two states, represented by the red and green curves, respectively, conﬁrming their edge-localized nature. Site-resolved (b) charge density ni = ni,↑+ ni,↓and (c) spin density mi = ni,↑−ni,↓plotted as a function of site index. The alternating red and blue dots in (b) and (c) distinguish adjacent site indices.

FIG. 3: (Color online). (a) Energy eigenvalue spectrum as a function of eigenstate index for a 100-site half-ﬁlled SSH chain with t1 = 0.5 and t2 = 1, in the presence of both on-site and nearest-neighbor Hubbard interactions U = 2 and V = 0.85. The isolated state below the lower band is marked by a red dot, and the in-gap state is marked by a green dot. The insets show the corresponding local probability amplitudes |ψi|2 for these two states, represented by the red and green curves, re- spectively. Site-resolved (b) charge density ni and (c) spin density mi plotted as a function of site index. The alternat- ing red and blue dots in (b) and (c) distinguish adjacent site indices.

FIG. 4: (Color online). (a) Energy eigenvalue spectrum as a function of eigenstate index for a 100-site half-ﬁlled SSH chain with t1 = 0.5 and t2 = 1, in the presence of both on-site and nearest-neighbor Hubbard interactions U = 0.8 and V = 0.6. The single in-gap state is marked by a green dot. The right inset shows the local probability amplitude |ψi|2 for the spin- up case (green curve), while the right inset shows the same for the spin-down case (red curve). Site-resolved (b) charge density ni and (c) spin density mi plotted as a function of site index. The alternating red and blue dots in (b) and (c) distinguish adjacent site indices.

FIG. 5: (Color online). (a) Energy eigenvalue spectrum as a function of eigenstate index for a 100-site half-ﬁlled SSH chain with t1 = 0.5 and t2 = 1, in the presence of both on-site and nearest-neighbor Hubbard interactions U = 2 and V = 1.5. The in-gap states are marked by blue dots. The inset shows the corresponding local probability amplitudes |ψi|2. Site- resolved (b) charge density ni and (c) spin density mi plotted as a function of site index. The alternating red and blue dots in (b) and (c) distinguish adjacent site indices.
